title: knotankey — Soft Luxury Crochet E-Commerce Frontend

requirements.summary: Set up React Router with all required routes, implement global loading screen with yarn animation, and ensure smooth page transitions
acceptanceCriteria: All routes render their respective pages without errors
Loading screen appears on initial load and fades out smoothly
Navigation between pages uses smooth transitions
App is fully responsive on mobile, tablet, and desktop

file_operations.description: Configure React Router with routes for /, /products, /products/:id, /cart, /checkout, /checkout/thank-you, /custom-order, /returns, and /admin. Add route transition animations using Framer Motion or CSS transitions for smooth page changes.

Create a global loading screen component with a spinning yarn animation (animated SVG or CSS keyframes) that displays on initial app load and fades out smoothly after a brief delay. Use soft luxury styling matching the brand aesthetic.
Create a custom hook to manage the loading screen state, triggering the fade-out after initial app mount and any necessary data preloading.

requirements.summary: Apply consistent soft-luxury visual theme with beige/cream palette, elegant typography, subtle textures, rounded corners, soft shadows, and premium hover animations
acceptanceCriteria: Beige/cream color palette is used consistently on all pages
Serif font used for all headings, sans-serif for body
All interactive elements have smooth hover transitions
Cards have soft shadows and rounded corners
No abrupt or jarring animations anywhere in the UI

file_operations.description: Define CSS custom properties for the soft luxury color palette (#FAF7F2, #F5EFE6 and related tones), import Google Fonts for Playfair Display (serif headings) and Lato or DM Sans (sans-serif body), set global font families, add subtle linen/fabric texture background using a repeating pattern or CSS filter, define transition durations for smooth animations (0.3s-0.5s), and establish base styles for rounded corners and soft shadows.

Extend Tailwind theme with custom colors (beige, cream, warm-white variants), custom font families (serif for headings, sans for body), custom border radius values for soft rounded corners, custom box shadow utilities for soft luxury shadows, and custom animation/transition timing functions for slow, elegant hover effects.

Create a reusable Card component with soft shadows, rounded corners, and smooth hover lift animation (slight translateY and scale). This component will be used for product cards, order cards, and other content cards throughout the app.

requirements.summary: Build Homepage with parallax hero, floating yarn animation, Best Sellers section, About section with scroll fade-in, and CTA banner
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section is full viewport height with parallax scroll effect
Floating yarn/particle animation is visible and subtle in the hero
Bestsellers section fetches and displays 4 bestseller products from the backend
About section fades in on scroll
All sections are visible and correctly laid out on mobile

file_operations.description: Build the Homepage with (1) HeroSection component rendering full-viewport parallax hero with background image from frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png, large 'knotankey' heading, tagline, and Shop Now CTA; (2) BestSellersSection fetching bestseller products from backend and displaying 4 cards; (3) AboutSection with fade-in on scroll animation; (4) CTABanner with 'Explore All Products' button. Wire the Shop Now and Explore buttons to navigate to /products.

Create the hero section component with full-viewport height, parallax scroll effect using CSS transform or a library like react-scroll-parallax, background image from frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png, brand heading 'knotankey', tagline 'Soft Luxury, Handcrafted With Love', and Shop Now button. Layer FloatingYarnAnimation component over the hero background.

Create a subtle floating/drifting yarn or orb particle animation using CSS keyframes or canvas-based animation. The animation should be slow, elegant, and non-distracting, layered over the hero section background.

Create BestSellersSection component that fetches products from backend using getProducts(), filters for bestseller=true, takes the first 4, and renders them using ProductCard components. Include section heading 'Best Sellers'.

Create AboutSection with background texture from frontend/public/assets/generated/about-bg.dim_1200x600.png, the text 'knotankey creates soft luxury crocheted pieces designed to bring warmth, comfort, and elegance into everyday life.', and a fade-in animation triggered on scroll using Intersection Observer or a library like react-intersection-observer.
Create CTABanner component with text 'Wrap Yourself in Handmade Luxury' and an 'Explore All Products' button that navigates to /products using React Router.

Create a reusable ProductCard component displaying product image (with hover zoom effect using CSS transform scale), title, price, Quick Add to Cart button (adds 1 item to cart with animation), and View Product button (navigates to /products/:id). Use the Card component for base styling and add product-specific hover interactions.

requirements.summary: Build All Products page with responsive grid, category/price/bestseller filters, sorting options, and client-side filtering logic
acceptanceCriteria: All products are fetched from the backend and displayed in a grid
Category filter narrows the displayed products correctly
Price range filter works correctly
Bestseller toggle filters to bestseller-only products
Sorting options reorder the grid correctly
Grid is responsive (1 col mobile, 2 col tablet, 3-4 col desktop)

file_operations.description: Build the All Products page fetching all products from backend using getProducts(). Implement client-side filtering by category (dropdown), price range (min/max inputs or slider), and bestseller toggle. Implement sorting by price (low to high, high to low) and newest. Display filtered/sorted products in a responsive grid using ProductCard components. Grid should be 1 column on mobile, 2 columns on tablet, 3-4 columns on desktop.

Create ProductFilters component with controls for category dropdown, price range inputs/slider, and bestseller toggle. Emit filter changes to parent component via callbacks. Style filters with soft luxury aesthetic.
Create ProductSort component with dropdown for sorting options: Price low to high, Price high to low, Newest. Emit sort selection to parent component via callback.
Create custom hook to manage product filtering and sorting logic. Accept full product list, filter criteria, and sort option; return filtered and sorted products array.

requirements.summary: Build Product Detail page displaying product info, large image with hover zoom, quantity selector, Add to Cart button with animation, and Buy Now button
acceptanceCriteria: Product data is loaded from the backend by ID
Quantity selector cannot go below 1
Add to Cart adds the correct quantity to the cart and shows a confirmation animation
Buy Now adds to cart and redirects to /checkout
Image zoom is smooth on hover

file_operations.description: Build Product Detail page that fetches product by ID from backend using getProductById(). Display large product image (from product.image.getDirectURL()) with smooth zoom on hover, product title, price, description, quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min value 1), Add to Cart button with elegant animation on click (using cart context), and Buy Now button that adds to cart and navigates to /checkout. Handle loading and error states.

Create QuantitySelector component with increment/decrement buttons and numeric display. Enforce minimum value of 1. Emit quantity changes to parent via callback. Style with soft luxury aesthetic and smooth hover transitions.
Create ProductImage component that displays a large product image with smooth zoom effect on hover using CSS transform scale and transition. Accept image URL as prop.

requirements.summary: Implement cart system with React Context and localStorage persistence, build Cart page, and create slide-in cart drawer with navbar integration
acceptanceCriteria: Cart persists across page refreshes via localStorage
Cart page shows all items with correct subtotals and total
Quantity changes in cart update totals in real time
Removing an item removes it from the cart
Slide-in cart drawer animates smoothly from the right
Cart icon in navbar shows item count badge

file_operations.description: Create CartContext with React Context API to manage cart state (items array with productId, title, price, image, quantity). Implement addToCart, updateQuantity, removeFromCart, clearCart functions. Persist cart state to localStorage and load on mount. Provide cart item count and total price computed values. Export CartProvider and useCart hook.

Build Cart page displaying all cart items using CartItem components. Show product image, title, price, quantity selector, remove button, and subtotal for each item. Display cart total at bottom with Proceed to Checkout button navigating to /checkout. Show empty cart message when cart is empty.

Create CartItem component displaying a single cart item with product image (from ExternalBlob.getDirectURL()), title, price, quantity selector (using QuantitySelector component), remove button, and subtotal calculation. Wire quantity changes and removal to CartContext functions.

Create CartDrawer component that slides in from the right side with smooth animation (using CSS transform translateX or Framer Motion). Display cart items summary, total, and buttons for View Cart (navigate to /cart) and Checkout (navigate to /checkout). Include close button. Component should be controlled by open/close state passed from parent.
Create custom hook to manage cart drawer open/close state and provide toggle function. This will be used by Navbar and other components that need to open the cart drawer.

requirements.summary: Build Checkout page with customer info form, order summary, mocked payment with Stripe placeholder, and Thank You page with order confirmation
acceptanceCriteria: All form fields are validated before submission
Order is saved to the backend on submission
Cart is cleared after successful order submission
User is redirected to /checkout/thank-you after placing order
Thank You page shows a confirmation message with order details
Stripe integration point is clearly commented in the code

file_operations.description: Build Checkout page with two-column layout: left column with CheckoutForm component collecting customer info (Full Name, Email, Phone, Shipping Address, City, Postal Code, Country) with validation; right column with OrderSummary component showing cart items, quantities, prices, and total. Include Place Order button that validates form, creates order in backend using createOrder(), clears cart, and redirects to /checkout/thank-you. Add clearly commented placeholder section for Stripe integration: /* STRIPE INTEGRATION POINT: Replace mocked payment logic here with Stripe Elements and payment intent creation */.

Create CheckoutForm component with form fields for Full Name, Email, Phone, Shipping Address, City, Postal Code, Country. Implement validation for all required fields (email format, phone format, non-empty fields). Use React Hook Form or controlled component pattern. Emit validated form data to parent via callback on submission.
Create OrderSummary component displaying cart items from CartContext with product names, quantities, prices, and subtotals. Show cart total at bottom. Style as a card with soft luxury aesthetic.

Build Thank You page showing order confirmation message with elegant animation (fade-in or scale-in). Display order ID (from URL params or state), confirmation text, and a button to return to homepage or continue shopping. Use soft luxury styling with celebratory but elegant tone.

Create custom hook to handle checkout submission logic. Accept customer info and cart data, construct Order object matching backend interface (generate order ID, map cart items to OrderItem[], calculate total, set initial status), call backend.createOrder(), and return submission state (loading, success, error).

requirements.summary: Build Custom Order page with form collecting product type, color, size, description, image upload (convert to base64), budget range, and email; submit to backend
acceptanceCriteria: All form fields are present and validated
Image upload converts the file to base64 before submission
Submission saves the custom order request in the backend
A success message is shown after successful submission
Form resets after successful submission

file_operations.description: Build Custom Order page with heading 'Design Your Own Crochet Piece'. Include CustomOrderForm component with fields for Product type (dropdown: bag, blanket, sweater, decor), Color preference (text input), Size (text input), Description (textarea), Inspiration image upload (file input with image preview), Budget range (dropdown or selector), Email (text input with validation). On submit, convert uploaded image to base64 using ExternalBlob.fromBytes(), construct CustomOrderRequest object, call backend.createCustomOrderRequest(), show success message, and reset form.

Create CustomOrderForm component with all required fields: product type dropdown, color preference input, size input, description textarea, inspiration image file upload with preview and base64 conversion using ExternalBlob.fromBytes(), budget range selector, and email input. Implement validation for required fields and email format. Emit validated form data (including ExternalBlob) to parent via callback on submission.

Create ImageUpload component with file input, image preview, and base64 conversion logic. Accept file, convert to Uint8Array, create ExternalBlob using ExternalBlob.fromBytes(), display preview using getDirectURL(), and emit ExternalBlob to parent via callback. Style with soft luxury aesthetic and include drag-and-drop support if feasible.

Create custom hook to handle custom order submission. Accept form data with ExternalBlob, construct CustomOrderRequest object (generate ID, include all fields), call backend.createCustomOrderRequest(), and return submission state (loading, success, error).

requirements.summary: Build Returns page with policy section and return request form submitting to backend
acceptanceCriteria: Policy content is clearly displayed
Return request form is fully functional with validation
Submission saves the request to the backend
Confirmation message appears after successful submission

file_operations.description: Build Returns page with two sections: (1) ReturnPolicy component displaying policy text (7-day return policy, unused handmade items only, custom orders non-refundable, contact email); (2) ReturnRequestForm component with fields for Order ID, Email, Reason (dropdown), Description (textarea). On submit, construct ReturnRequest object, call backend.createReturnRequest(), show confirmation message, and reset form.

Create ReturnPolicy component displaying the return policy content in an elegant, readable format. Include policy points: 7-day return policy, handmade items must be unused, custom orders non-refundable, and contact email. Style with soft luxury aesthetic.

Create ReturnRequestForm component with fields for Order ID (text input), Email (text input with validation), Reason (dropdown with common reasons), and Description (textarea). Implement validation for required fields and email format. Emit validated form data to parent via callback on submission.

Create custom hook to handle return request submission. Accept form data, construct ReturnRequest object (generate ID and timestamp), call backend.createReturnRequest(), and return submission state (loading, success, error).

requirements.summary: Build Admin Panel as protected route with passcode gate, showing dashboard tabs for Products CRUD, Orders with status updates, Custom Order Requests viewer, and Return Requests list
acceptanceCriteria: Passcode gate blocks access without correct password
Incorrect passcode shows an error message
Products tab allows full CRUD with backend calls
Bestseller toggle updates the product in the backend
Orders tab shows all orders and allows status updates
Custom orders tab shows all requests including image previews
Return requests tab lists all submissions

file_operations.description: Build Admin Panel page with initial AdminPasscodeGate component. On successful authentication (passcode 'knotankey_admin_2026'), store auth state in React state (not localStorage) and show AdminDashboard component with tabs/sections for Products, Orders, Custom Order Requests, and Return Requests. Each tab renders its respective component.

Create AdminPasscodeGate component with password input field and submit button. On submit, check if entered passcode equals 'knotankey_admin_2026'. If correct, call onAuthenticated callback; if incorrect, show error message. Style with soft luxury aesthetic.

Create AdminDashboard component with tab navigation for Products, Orders, Custom Order Requests, and Return Requests. Render active tab content using ProductsManagement, OrdersManagement, CustomOrdersViewer, and ReturnRequestsViewer components. Pass authenticated passcode to child components for backend calls.

Create ProductsManagement component fetching all products from backend using getProducts(). Display product list with edit/delete/bestseller toggle actions. Include AddProductForm for creating new products. All mutations (createProduct, updateProduct, deleteProduct) pass the passcode parameter and handle backend errors. Use ExternalBlob.fromBytes() for product image uploads.

Create AddProductForm component with fields for title, description, price, category, image upload (using ExternalBlob.fromBytes()), and bestseller checkbox. On submit, construct Product object with generated ID, call backend.createProduct(passcode, product), show success/error message, and reset form.

Create EditProductForm component similar to AddProductForm but pre-filled with existing product data. On submit, call backend.updateProduct(passcode, product) with updated values, show success/error message, and close form.

Create OrdersManagement component fetching all orders from backend using getOrders(passcode). Display order list showing order ID, customer info, items, total, current status, and a status update dropdown. On status change, call backend.updateOrderStatus(passcode, orderId, newStatus) and refresh order list.

Create CustomOrdersViewer component fetching all custom order requests from backend using getCustomOrderRequests(passcode). Display list showing request ID, product type, color preference, size, description, budget range, email, and image preview (using inspirationImage.getDirectURL()). Style as cards with soft luxury aesthetic.

Create ReturnRequestsViewer component fetching all return requests from backend using getReturnRequests(passcode). Display list showing request ID, order ID, email, reason, description, and submission date. Style as cards with soft luxury aesthetic.

Create custom hook to manage admin authentication state (boolean flag and passcode string stored in React state only). Provide authenticate function to validate and store passcode, and logout function to clear auth state. Do not persist to localStorage.

requirements.summary: Build sticky navbar with brand logo, navigation links, cart icon with badge, music toggle; build footer with social links, contact info, policy links, and newsletter signup
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar is sticky and visible on all pages
Cart icon shows current item count
Music toggle button is visible in the navbar
Footer contains social links, contact, policy links, and newsletter form
Newsletter form submits email to backend and shows confirmation

file_operations.description: Create Navbar component that is sticky/fixed at top with soft frosted-glass or slightly opaque warm background. Include 'knotankey' brand logo/wordmark (from frontend/public/assets/generated/logo.dim_400x120.png) on left, navigation links (Home, Products, Custom Order, Returns) in center using React Router Link components, and icons on right for cart (with item count badge from CartContext) and music toggle (using MusicToggle component). On cart icon click, open CartDrawer. Wire all navigation links to their respective routes.

Create Footer component with social links (Instagram, Pinterest as external links with icons), contact info (email), policy links (Returns navigating to /returns), and NewsletterSignup component. Style with soft luxury aesthetic and appropriate spacing.

Create NewsletterSignup component with email input and Subscribe button. On submit, validate email format, call backend.subscribeToNewsletter(email), show success confirmation message, and reset input. Handle backend errors gracefully.
Wrap all route content with Navbar at top and Footer at bottom to ensure they appear on every page. Update route structure to include the layout components.

requirements.summary: Implement background music toggle UI wired to HTML audio element with placeholder audio file path, toggle play/pause with smooth icon transition, no autoplay
acceptanceCriteria: Music toggle button is visible in the navbar
Clicking toggle plays/pauses the audio
Audio does not autoplay on page load
A clear code comment marks the placeholder audio file path
Icon changes between playing and muted states

file_operations.description: Create MusicToggle component with a button showing a musical note or speaker icon. Wire to an HTML audio element with src='/assets/music/background.mp3'. Implement toggle functionality switching between playing and paused states. Icon should transition smoothly between playing (unmuted) and muted states. Audio must NOT autoplay on page load. Include comment: /* AUDIO FILE PLACEHOLDER: Place your background music file at public/assets/music/background.mp3 */

Create custom hook to manage audio playback state (playing/paused) and provide play/pause toggle function. Use useRef to hold the audio element reference. Return current playing state and toggle function.

requirements.summary: Wire generated product images, hero background, about background, and logo into frontend assets and components
acceptanceCriteria: All 8 product cards display their respective warm, crochet-aesthetic images
Hero section has a large, warm, luxury crochet background image
About section has a soft fabric/textile background
Images load correctly and are not broken

file_operations.description: Create a module exporting seed product data matching the 8 sample products. Include product IDs, titles, descriptions, prices, categories, bestseller flags, and image paths using the workspace paths: frontend/public/assets/generated/product-blanket.dim_600x600.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/product-handbag.dim_600x600.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/product-shawl.dim_600x600.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/product-cushion.dim_600x600.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/product-tote.dim_600x600.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/product-scarf.dim_600x600.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/product-booties.dim_600x600.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/product-throw.dim_600x600.png. This data structure will be used to reference product images throughout the app before backend is seeded.

Update HeroSection to use the hero background image from the exact workspace path: frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png. Set this as the background-image CSS property or img src for the parallax background.

Update AboutSection to use the about background image from the exact workspace path: frontend/public/assets/generated/about-bg.dim_1200x600.png. Set this as the background-image CSS property for the section.
Update Navbar to use the logo image from the exact workspace path: frontend/public/assets/generated/logo.dim_400x120.png. Set this as the src for the logo/wordmark image element.
